_README: HSF VOTER GUIDE - SEED DATA FILE
This file is the entire content of the guide. The app renders it and adds nothing.
Edit this file to change the guide. No code changes required.
ALL NAMES BELOW ARE INVENTED SAMPLE CONTENT. Replace before publishing.
Delete the demo_notice line in settings to remove the sample-content banner.
Validate edits at jsonlint.com before publishing - one missing comma blanks the site.

settings.tagline: Hold the Line. Seize New Ground. Fight Like Hell.
settings.demo_notice: Everything on this page is invented sample content, shown to demonstrate the format. No real candidate or organization is listed, and nothing here is an endorsement.
settings.publisher_name: TODO - registered name of the publishing organization
settings.paid_for_by: TODO - required disclaimer string, exact wording from counsel
settings.methodology_note: TODO - two or three sentences on how entries are chosen. Readers trust a guide that shows its work. Say who decides, what they look at, and how someone suggests a change.

categories.blurb: Incumbents in office who are doing the work. Defend these seats.
Candidates committed to going further than the seat's current occupant.
Organizations on the front lines. Show up, give, volunteer.

races.notes: Statewide race - appears on every county ballot.
Chief elections officer for the state.
Regulates utilities. Multi-seat race - confirm how many seats are up this cycle.
Verify district boundaries and county coverage against current maps.
Spans more than one county - both are listed so it appears on both filters.
Arizona has 30 legislative districts. Some cross county lines.
Each legislative district elects two House members. Voters may select two.
Sheriff and County Attorney are the two local offices most directly tied to how immigration enforcement cooperation plays out on the ground.
The Recorder runs voter registration and early ballots.
At-large council seats. Confirm how many are up.

races.office: Tucson Unified School District Governing Board
races.notes: School board races are usually the lowest-information, highest-leverage item on the ballot.

entries.name: Adriana Vasquez-Hall
entries.headline: The veto pen is the whole job right now.
entries.summary: Sample copy. She has blocked eleven bills that would have restricted early voting, expanded cooperation between local police and federal immigration agents, or stripped local governments of the ability to set their own labor standards. None of that is glamorous. All of it held.
why_bullets: Vetoed the 2025 bill requiring county sheriffs to honor federal detainer requests
Restored full funding to the state housing trust fund after a decade of raids on it
Kept the drought contingency negotiations at the table rather than in court

entries.name: Marcus Bell
entries.headline: Certified the count under threat and did not blink.
entries.summary: Sample copy. He certified results on schedule in 2024 while under sustained pressure not to, published the full audit trail unprompted, and has kept the voter file out of the hands of every outside group that has asked for it.
why_bullets: Published county-level audit results within 72 hours of certification
Refused four separate requests for bulk voter file transfers
Expanded ballot drop locations on tribal land from 9 to 24

entries.name: Daniel Ruiz Ortega
entries.headline: One of the few willing to say the quiet part on the border.
entries.summary: Sample copy. He represents a district that runs from Tucson to the border and has spent four years refusing the easy vote on enforcement funding. He has also delivered - the Douglas port modernization is his.
why_bullets: Voted against the 2025 detention capacity expansion
Secured funding for the Douglas port of entry rebuild
Holds in-person town halls in Cochise County, not just Tucson

entries.name: Priya Raman
entries.headline: Held a one-vote margin and used it.
entries.summary: Sample copy. In a chamber decided by a single seat, she has been the vote that stopped the school voucher expansion twice. Her district is winnable by the other side and she has never softened to keep it.
why_bullets: Blocked the voucher expansion in committee two sessions running
Wrote the rural broadband bill that passed with bipartisan support
Only legislator to vote against the utility rate settlement

entries.name: Naomi Betsuie
entries.headline: Declined to prosecute what she was never required to prosecute.
entries.summary: Sample copy. She ended cash bail requests for non-violent misdemeanors in her first month and has not reversed under pressure. Charging data is published quarterly, which is rare and worth defending.
why_bullets: Publishes charging and declination data every quarter
Ended cash bail requests for non-violent misdemeanors
Created the conviction integrity unit that has vacated six sentences

entries.name: Frank Tsosie
entries.headline: Got polling places onto the reservation and kept them there.
entries.summary: Sample copy. Four years of steady work on a board where he is often outvoted. The early voting sites in the northern part of the county exist because he refused to let the item drop off three consecutive agendas.
why_bullets: Added four early voting locations in the north county
Pushed through the wildfire evacuation route funding
Voted against the short-term rental exemption

entries.name: Winona Charley
entries.headline: The seat has never been contested this seriously.
entries.summary: Sample copy. She has organized in the northern part of this district for eleven years and is running on water rights and rural health care in a place where both are urgent. The incumbent has not held a public town hall since 2023.
why_bullets: Would be the first person from the northern counties to hold this seat
Campaign is field-first, with offices already open in three towns
Committed to a moratorium on new groundwater pumping permits

entries.name: Terrence Okoye
entries.headline: Would go further than the seat has ever gone on housing.
entries.summary: Sample copy. The current member votes the right way on housing and stops there. Okoye has actually written the policy - he drafted the Tucson tenant protection ordinance and knows what the state preemption fight requires.
why_bullets: Drafted the city tenant protection ordinance now in force
Supports repealing state preemption of local rent stabilization
Would be the only renter in the delegation

entries.name: Rosa Delgado
entries.headline: The clearest break available on any ballot in this county.
entries.summary: Sample copy. Twenty-two years in the department and running against her own leadership on one question: whether the county jail holds people for federal immigration agents. She says no. The incumbent says it is complicated.
why_bullets: Would end all detainer holds without a judicial warrant
Committed to publishing every use-of-force report within 30 days
Would close the jail's contract housing agreement

entries.name: Simone Aguilar
entries.headline: The largest prosecutor's office in the state, and it is winnable.
entries.summary: Sample copy. A former public defender running for the office that has spent a decade setting the tone for criminal justice across Arizona. She has named the specific units she would dissolve, which almost no challenger does.
why_bullets: Would dissolve the gang designation unit
Committed to declining prosecution of all abortion-related cases
Would open the charging data that has never been published

entries.name: Elena Marquez
entries.headline: A border city choosing what kind of border city it is.
entries.summary: Sample copy. Running on water infrastructure and small business support in a town where the current administration has spent three years on enforcement theater. Local races in Cochise County are decided by a few hundred votes.
why_bullets: Would end the city's participation in the federal task force agreement
Plan to replace the aging water main system rather than defer it again
Ran the downtown merchants association through the 2024 closures

entries.name: Amina Kowalski
entries.headline: School board turnout is about nine percent. That is the opportunity.
entries.summary: Sample copy. A district parent and former classroom aide running against a slate that has spent two years on library content reviews. She wants the conversation back on class sizes and the teacher vacancy rate.
why_bullets: Opposes the book review policy adopted last year
Would restore the ethnic studies elective
Committed to publishing the vacancy rate by school every month

organizations.name: Sonoran Rapid Response Network
organizations.description: Sample copy. A 24-hour hotline and verified responder network. When someone is detained, trained volunteers document the stop, notify family, and connect the household to legal help within the hour.
ways_to_help: Train as a legal observer - one Saturday session
Take a shift on the hotline, remote and from home
Give to the bond fund, which is always the binding constraint

organizations.name: Borderlands Legal Aid Project
organizations.description: Sample copy. Two staff attorneys and a rotating bench of volunteer lawyers covering the whole southeastern corner of the state. They take the cases nobody else in the region can take.
ways_to_help: Volunteer if you are a licensed attorney or a Spanish interpreter
Drive clients to hearings in Tucson and Phoenix
Sponsor a single case - roughly the cost of a filing fee

organizations.name: High Desert Native Vote
organizations.description: Sample copy. Registration and ballot access work across the northern counties, where a round trip to a polling place can take four hours. They run the shuttles, the translation, and the drop box monitoring.
ways_to_help: Drive a ballot shuttle route on election day
Register voters at community events, training provided
Fund a fuel stipend for a rural canvasser

organizations.name: Valley Voter Defense Fund
organizations.description: Sample copy. Poll monitors, a same-day legal hotline, and the people who show up when a drop box is being watched by someone with a camera and a folding chair.
ways_to_help: Serve as a trained poll monitor for one shift
Give to cover the cost of election-day operations

organizations.name: Arizona Doorstep Coalition
organizations.description: Sample copy. The canvass infrastructure that the smaller groups all borrow. Turf, training, and a data operation that does not sell what it collects.
ways_to_help: Take a weekend canvass shift in your own neighborhood
Host a turf launch - coffee and a table is all it takes
Give monthly, which is what lets them hire organizers year round

organizations.name: Old Pueblo Tenant Union
organizations.description: Sample copy. Eviction court accompaniment, building-level organizing, and an emergency fund that has kept a few hundred households in place. Most of their work happens the week before a hearing.
ways_to_help: Accompany a neighbor to eviction court, no legal training needed
Join a building committee where you already live
Give to the emergency rent fund
